metadata.description: Essential Chinese vocabulary for daily routines, everyday activities, and personal habits

content.name: measure_word_for_essays_articles_etc
content.side_e: Wǒ xiě le yì piān rìjì.
content.side_f: I wrote a diary entry. (lit. one [MW] diary)
content.side_g: 篇 is the measure word for discrete written pieces—diary entries, essays, articles, chapters—not for books (本) or letters (封). Always pair with 一/两/这/那 + 篇 + noun.

content.name: diary
content.side_e: Tā měi tiān wǎnshang xiě rìjì.
content.side_f: He writes a diary every night.
content.side_g: 日记 literally 'day-record.' Typical verbs: 写日记 (write), 记日记 (keep). Counted with 篇 for individual entries or 本 for the notebook itself. Neutral register, both spoken and written.

content.name: tired
content.side_e: Jīntiān gōngzuò tài duō, wǒ hěn lèi.
content.side_f: There's too much work today; I'm very tired.
content.side_g: 累 (lèi) as an adjective needs 很/太/非常 in affirmatives to avoid sounding comparative. Distinguish from 困 (kùn, sleepy) and 辛苦 (xīnkǔ, arduous). 你累不累? is a common A-not-A form.

content.name: vo_to_get_up
content.side_e: Wǒ měi tiān qī diǎn qǐchuáng.
content.side_f: I get up at seven every day. (lit. rise-bed)
content.side_g: 起床 is a verb-object (离合词 separable) compound: you can say 起了床, 起过床, or 起不了床, but not *起床了我. Means literally 'rise from bed,' i.e., get out of bed—not simply wake up (that's 醒 xǐng).

content.name: vo_to_take_a_bath_shower
content.side_e: Tā qǐchuáng yǐhòu jiù qù xǐzǎo.
content.side_f: After getting up he goes to shower. (lit. wash-bath)
content.side_g: 洗澡 is another separable verb-object: 洗了一个澡, 洗个热水澡. Covers both bathing and showering; specify with 淋浴 (shower) or 泡澡 (soak) if needed. Neutral register.

content.name: breakfast
content.side_e: Zǎofàn nǐ xiǎng chī shénme?
content.side_f: What do you want for breakfast?
content.side_g: 早饭 is the colloquial term; 早餐 (zǎocān) is slightly more formal and common on menus. Verb: 吃早饭. Parallel set: 早饭/午饭/晚饭 vs. formal 早餐/午餐/晚餐.

content.name: simultaneously_at_the_same_time
content.side_e: Wǒ yìbiān chī zǎofàn, yìbiān kàn shū.
content.side_f: I eat breakfast and read at the same time.
content.side_g: Standard pronunciation is yìbiān (tone sandhi from yī). The grammar pattern 一边 V1，一边 V2 expresses two actions performed concurrently by the same subject.

content.name: classroom
content.side_e: Lǎoshī yǐjīng zài jiàoshì li le.
content.side_f: The teacher is already in the classroom.
content.side_g: 教室 (jiàoshì) vs. 教师 (jiàoshī, teacher) differ only in tone—take care. Use 在教室里 for location. Classifier: 间 (yì jiān jiàoshì).

content.name: pronunciation
content.side_e: Tā de Zhōngwén fāyīn hěn hǎo.
content.side_f: His Chinese pronunciation is very good.
content.side_g: Standard pinyin is fāyīn (1st+1st). Common collocations: 练发音 (practice), 纠正发音 (correct), 发音标准 (standard pronunciation). Contrast 口音 kǒuyīn 'accent.'

content.name: new
content.side_e: Wǒ mǎi le yí ge xīn diànnǎo.
content.side_f: I bought a new computer.
content.side_g: 新 is attributive and needs no 的 before most nouns (新书, 新朋友). As predicate, needs 很: 这本书很新. Opposite 旧 jiù. In 新年 'New Year' it's fixed.

content.name: computer
content.side_e: Wǒ de xīn diànnǎo hěn kuài.
content.side_f: My new computer is very fast. (lit. electric-brain)
content.side_g: 电脑 is the colloquial standard; 计算机 jìsuànjī is the formal/technical term. Measure word 台 (yì tái diànnǎo). Common verbs: 用, 开, 关, 修.

content.name: noon
content.side_e: Zhōngwǔ wǒmen yìqǐ qù chī wǔfàn, hǎo ma?
content.side_f: At noon let's go eat lunch together, okay?
content.side_g: 中午 covers roughly 11 a.m.–1 p.m. Fits the time-word slot before the verb: 我中午吃饭, not after. Parallel set: 早上/上午/中午/下午/晚上.

content.name: dining_room_cafeteria
content.side_e: Xuéxiào de cāntīng bú tài guì.
content.side_f: The school cafeteria isn't too expensive.
content.side_g: Correct pinyin is cāntīng (1st+1st). 餐厅 is any dining hall or restaurant room; a student cafeteria is often 食堂 shítáng, which is more informal/campus-specific.

content.name: lunch_midday_meal
content.side_e: Wǒ zhōngwǔ zài cāntīng chī wǔfàn.
content.side_f: I eat lunch in the cafeteria at noon.
content.side_g: 午饭 pairs with 吃. More formal equivalent: 午餐. Don't confuse with 午觉 wǔjiào 'midday nap.' Time-place-verb order: 中午 + 在餐厅 + 吃午饭.

content.name: vo_to_go_online_to_surf_the_internet
content.side_a: to go online; to surf the internet
content.side_e: Tā měi tiān wǎnshang dōu shàngwǎng.
content.side_f: He goes online every evening.
content.side_g: 上网 is separable: 上了一个小时网, 上不了网 'can't get online.' Use 用电脑上网 or 用手机上网 to specify device. Modern alternative: 刷手机/刷微博 for casual browsing.

content.name: dormitory
content.side_e: Wǒ de sùshè lí jiàoshì hěn jìn.
content.side_f: My dorm is close to the classroom.
content.side_g: 宿舍 (sùshè) is standard for dormitory; measure word 间 for a room, 栋/座 for the building. Distance pattern A 离 B 很近/远 is the canonical frame.

content.name: there
content.side_e: Lǎoshī zài nàr, nǐ qù wèn tā ba.
content.side_f: The teacher is there—go ask him.
content.side_g: 那儿 (nàr, Beijing/northern erhua) and 那里 (nàlǐ, southern/written) are interchangeable. Use after verbs of location/motion: 在那儿, 去那儿, 从那儿来. Contrast 这儿 zhèr 'here,' 哪儿 nǎr 'where.'

content.name: in_the_middle_of_doing_something
content.side_e: Tā zhèngzài jiàoshì li liànxí fāyīn.
content.side_f: She is in the middle of practicing pronunciation in the classroom.
content.side_g: 正在 marks ongoing action, often with sentence-final 呢: 正在…呢. Compare 在 (simple progressive), 正 (just at that moment), 正在 (emphasizes both 'right now' and 'in progress'). Precedes the verb directly; cannot take 了 or 过.

content.name: before
content.side_e: Yǐqián wǒ bù zhīdao tā yǒu xīn diànnǎo.
content.side_f: I didn't know before that he had a new computer.
content.side_g: 以前 can stand alone ('in the past') or follow a phrase ('before X'): 吃饭以前 'before eating,' 三年以前 'three years ago.' Opposite 以后. In this use, no 的 is needed before 以前.

content.name: to_tell
content.side_e: Qǐng nǐ gàosu tā wǒ yǐjīng qǐchuáng le.
content.side_f: Please tell him I've already gotten up.
content.side_g: 告诉 takes a double object: 告诉 + 人 + 事 (告诉我答案). The su is neutral tone. Don't substitute 说 shuō, which doesn't take an indirect-object person directly (*说我… is wrong; say 跟我说). Common pattern: 告诉你一件事.

content.name: already
content.side_e: Wǒ yǐjīng chī le zǎofàn le.
content.side_f: I've already eaten breakfast.
content.side_g: 已经 typically pairs with sentence-final 了 to mark a completed/current-relevant state: 已经…了. Precedes the verb. Contrast with 才 cái 'only then / not until,' which implies late or limited.

content.name: to_know
content.side_e: Wǒ zhīdao tā zhèngzài xǐzǎo.
content.side_f: I know she's in the middle of showering.
content.side_g: 知道 (zhīdao, 2nd syllable neutral tone) = know a fact; contrast 认识 rènshi 'be acquainted with (person/place).' Negation: 不知道, not *没知道. Common fixed: 我知道了 'got it / understood.'
